<think>
I need to create a full-stack restaurant management application using Next.js 14, TypeScript, and the App Router.

Plan:
1.  Initialize the project structure and create the 24 files as requested.
2.  Use `task_tracker` to manage the creation of these files in batches.
3.  Use `str_replace_editor` with the `create` command for all file creations.
4.  Verify the structure and ensure all dependencies are included in `package.json`.

Files to create:
1.  `package.json`
2.  `tsconfig.json`
3.  `next.config.js`
4.  `prisma/schema.prisma`
5.  `src/app/layout.tsx`
6.  `src/app/page.tsx`
7.  `src/app/globals.css`
8.  `src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`
9.  `src/app/api/restaurants/route.ts`
10. `src/app/api/menu/route.ts`
11. `src/app/api/orders/route.ts`
12. `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`
13. `src/app/menu/page.tsx`
14. `src/app/orders/page.tsx`
15. `src/app/auth/login/page.tsx`
16. `src/app/auth/register/page.tsx`
17. `src/lib/prisma.ts`
18. `src/lib/auth.ts`
19. `src/components/Navbar.tsx`
20. `src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx`
21. `src/components/MenuItemForm.tsx`
22. `src/components/OrderTable.tsx`
23. `src/middleware.ts`
24. `.env.example`

I will start by setting up the task tracker.
</think>

task_list.description: Create configuration files (package.json, tsconfig.json, next.config.js, prisma/schema.prisma)
Create core library files (src/lib/prisma.ts, src/lib/auth.ts) and middleware (src/middleware.ts)
Create global styles and env template (src/app/globals.css, .env.example)
